title: Rollback Snapshot and Execution
outOfScope: deletion of project-owned business code or configuration

sources.reason: Rollback Snapshot and Rollback Execution acceptance and verification requirements.
Migration plan and partial rollback semantics consumed by rollback planning.
Installed manifest and managed-region fact source.
Upgrade apply ordering and snapshot boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: Rollback must fail closed on missing snapshots or drift and must preserve project-owned content.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized execution of every Work Item in the remediation plan, including governance changes.

preReviewWarnings: Rollback proposal must distinguish Runtime restore from project-owned preservation.
Missing snapshot, drift, and non-invertible migration must remain partial_rollback or blocked.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Snapshot planning records manifest, version, managed regions, runtime sources, project config hash, migration plan, and instructions.
Rollback validates current installation, requires confirmation, restores only owned Runtime and Managed Region content, and preserves project-owned content.
Missing snapshots, drift, non-invertible migration, and incomplete restoration fail closed with partial_rollback or blocked and a resume condition.
Tests and lifecycle documentation cover complete, drifted, missing, and partial rollback paths.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: An upgrade must be reversible without overwriting business code or project-owned configuration created or modified after the upgrade.
constraints: Snapshot storage is versioned by upgrade id and must include hashes and restoration instructions.
Rollback requires explicit confirmation, validates current installation facts, and never deletes or overwrites project-owned content.
Unsupported reverse migrations and missing evidence must be reported as partial_rollback or blocked.
intent.rationale: A deterministic proposal and fail-closed executor separates evidence collection from destructive restoration and makes residual manual work auditable.

rawUserRequest: Execute the installed lifecycle remediation plan serially; each Work Item must complete PR, merge, branch cleanup, and ai-close lifecycle before the next.

declaredIntent.summary: Implement and verify the rollback snapshot and execution Work Item within the repository.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Complete rollback restores owned Runtime and Managed Region content.
Drift or missing snapshot blocks without writes.
Non-invertible migration reports partial_rollback and residual instructions.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete serial remediation plan, including governance policy changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
